title: Elaeocarpus oxypyren
intro: Elaeocarpus oxypyren, commonly known as the Oxypetalum or Oxypetalus, is a plant species found in the family Elaeocarpaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Elaeocarpus oxypyren include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
